repo_prior_art.cite: none; the closest catalogue stat_policy_regret_margin_overlap has a policy-regret target, not honest interval length for an optimal value

published_prior_art.cite: WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, XuGuo2026, WuWang2021, and omitted ShiLuSong2020 establish optimal-value inference, but none inspected states this finite-union uniform-honesty/stratumwise-length theorem

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue entry studies confidence-interval expected length for an optimal-policy value

published_prior_art.cite: CaiLow2004 supplies ordered-modulus expected-length lower bounds for linear Gaussian functionals; the two-point testing step is an adaptation, not a new generic lower-bound principle
LuedtkeVanderLaan2016 analyzes exceptional-law optimal-value inference; the displayed rate balance and root-n plateau component are specialized reductions
CaiLow2004, XuGuo2026, WhitehouseAusternSyrgkanis2025, and ShiLuSong2020 do not state a causal ordered expected-length adaptation diagram jointly indexed by tie mass, margin, and nuisance smoothness

novelty_flags.label: oeq:uniform-honesty-upper-length / literature review
novelty_flags.one_line: Shi, Lu, and Song (2020), which gives nonregular optimal-value confidence intervals and an average-length comparison, is a closer omitted comparator; without distinguishing it, the published axis is borderline and the current not-publishable kernel remains below novelty_target=field.

soundness_flags.label: def:testing-modulus / thm:testing-modulus-lower-bound
soundness_flags.one_line: Because asymptotic honesty permits arbitrary behavior at any fixed n, for each n one can choose an otherwise honest interval sequence with C_n a point interval; hence \(\mathfrak L^{\mathrm{asy}}_{j,n}=0\) and the claimed positive liminf frontier/modulus lower bound cannot follow.

soundness_flags.label: ass:nonseparated-index / f_j / \kappa_j / lem:nonempty-strata
soundness_flags.one_line: The allowed equality \(\alpha_j=1/s_j\) with integer \(s_j\) makes \(f_j(x)=(x_1-\bar p_j)_+^{s_j}\) fail to lie in standard \(C^{s_j}\), so \(\lVert f_j\rVert_{C^{s_j}}\), \(\kappa_j\), and the universal feasibility witness are not defined as claimed.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:uniform-honesty-upper-length
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The proposed interval is conditional on known 1/2 randomization, bounded outcomes and design density, and the finite predeclared union; estimated propensities and coarsening are excluded.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any repaired honesty and length claim is only over \(\mathcal U\) under these declared primitives.

source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The article gives a necessary-and-sufficient pathwise-differentiability condition for the optimal value and describes root-n confidence intervals even when it is not pathwise differentiable.
The article studies a smoothed robust estimator and resampling-based asymptotically accurate inference for both an optimal-regime parameter and its value function.

The preprint develops adaptive smoothing for valid optimal-regime-value inference under nonregularity and claims an attained asymptotic-variance lower bound within robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimators.
The preprint constructs softmax-smoothed optimal-policy-value inference with a constant number of nuisance fits, without semiparametric restrictions, and efficiency under zero treatment non-response.
The article develops adaptive confidence intervals for linear functionals, using between-class and ordered moduli for expected-length lower bounds and constructions.
The article combines margin conditions, Hölder regression classes, and density regularity in plug-in classification-rate theory.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the pointwise infimum over asymptotically honest sequences by a finite-n uniformly honest decision class, or formulate one sequence-level asymptotic minimax criterion; then rederive the testing lower bound for that nondegenerate frontier.

Repair the nonseparated witness by requiring \(\alpha_j<1/s_j\) at integer smoothness or by replacing the power profile with an explicit smooth bump having the required margin law, and reprove nonemptiness.
Add and distinguish Shi, Lu, and Song (2020) alongside Whitehouse et al., specifying why their coverage/length results do not yield the finite-union ordered-modulus target.

recommended_next_step: Return to Stage -1 for a repair of the asymptotic frontier definition and the boundary Hölder witness, then reassess the field-tier causal adaptation kernel.
